name: Insalada Di Cavolfiore (Cauliflower Salad)
recipeIngredient: 1 teaspoon fresh thyme leaves, or 1/2 tsp. dried
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour enough water into a saucepan to cover the cauliflower generously and bring to a boil.
2. Meanwhile, trim the stalks off the cauliflower and cut off the hard base and discard. Cut the cauliflower into quarters lengthwise.
3. Add the cauliflower to the boiling water, along with the salt and lemon juice (the lemon juice will help the cauliflower retain its color). Cover, return to a boil, and continue to cook until the cauliflower is tender but not mushy, five to seven minutes. Handle carefully as cauliflower can quickly go from undercooked to overcooked.
4. In a small bowl, combine all dressing ingredients, including salt and pepper to taste. Whisk to blend.
5. When the cauliflower is cooked, drain immediately and plunge into cold water to stop further cooking. Drain again.
6. Break up the cauliflower into quite small florets so it combines easily with the dressing, and place in a salad bowl. If the florets are too large, the olives and capers may settle at the bottom of the salad bowl.
7. Pour the dressing over the top, toss well, and serve.
